accurate.why: The claims present (HJ Res 90, introduced April 3, 2025, by Rep. Bice, disapproving a CFTC rule) are all accurate per the bill text.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradicting claims appear in the partial summary.

states_purpose.why: The summary identifies the purpose as congressional disapproval and invalidation of a federal agency rule.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The summary is cut off before identifying the CFTC's voluntary carbon credit derivative guidance being disapproved under chapter 8 of title 5 (CRA).

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a simple disapproval resolution with no material exceptions or conditions.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective dates or deadlines beyond the disapproval itself.

major_provisions_covered.why: The summary truncates before naming the CFTC guidance and stating the rule shall have no force or effect, omitting the essential operative content.

objective_tone.why: The partial summary is neutral and non-partisan.

coherent.why: The summary ends mid-sentence ('issued by the'), making it incomplete and not internally complete.

concise.why: The text present is concise without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Entities named (HJ Res 90, Rep. Bice, the federal agency rule) are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The only figures stated (HJ Res 90, April 3, 2025) are correct per the bill text.
